facts.fact: The insurer is ProtectPlus Family Insurance Co.
The policy number is PP-TL-2024-6729R.
The policy date is July 12, 2024.
The policy type is 30-Year Level Premium Term.
The insured's name is Christopher David Bennett.
Christopher David Bennett's sex is Male.
Christopher David Bennett's date of birth is May 20, 1987.
Christopher David Bennett's issue age is 37.
Christopher David Bennett's underwriting class is Preferred Non-Smoker.
The face amount is $750,000.
The policy period is 30 years, expiring July 12, 2054.
The base annual premium is $612.00.
The total annual premium including riders is $1,089.00.
The primary beneficiary Rachel Marie Bennett (Wife) receives 60%.
The primary beneficiary Christopher Bennett Jr. (Son) receives 20%.
The primary beneficiary Olivia Grace Bennett (Daughter) receives 20%.
The contingent beneficiary is Bennett Family Irrevocable Trust at 100%.
Disability is defined as the complete inability to perform the material and substantial duties of the insured's regular occupation due to sickness or injury.
Terminal Illness is defined as a condition expected to result in death within 24 months as certified by a physician.
Chronic Illness is defined as the inability to perform at least 2 of 6 Activities of Daily Living for at least 90 days.
The insurer will pay the death benefit if the insured dies while the policy is in force and all premiums have been paid.
Death benefit payment will be made to the beneficiary within 30 days of receiving satisfactory proof of death.
If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of the issue date, the insurer will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.
Rider 1 is the Waiver of Premium Due to Disability rider with a premium of $102 per year.
Under Rider 1, if the insured becomes totally disabled before age 60 and remains disabled for 6 consecutive months, all future premiums are waived.
Under Rider 1, coverage continues in full force during the waiver period.
Under Rider 1, the waiver continues until the insured recovers, returns to work, or reaches age 65.
Under Rider 1, annual proof of continuing disability must be submitted.
Rider 2 is the Accelerated Death Benefit - Terminal Illness rider, included at no charge.
Under Rider 2, if diagnosed with a terminal illness, the insured may access up to 95% of the death benefit while living.
Under Rider 2, the minimum advance is $10,000.
Under Rider 2, the maximum advance is $500,000.
Under Rider 2, the benefit is reduced by an actuarial discount of approximately 8% per year.
Under Rider 2, the remaining death benefit is paid to beneficiaries upon death.
Rider 3 is the Accelerated Death Benefit - Chronic Illness rider with a premium of $48 per year.

Under Rider 3, if the insured becomes chronically ill and cannot perform 2 or more Activities of Daily Living (bathing, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring, continence), they may access up to 2% of the face amount per month.
Under Rider 3, the maximum total advance is 50% of the face amount.
Under Rider 3, no actuarial discount is applied.
Under Rider 3, the chronic illness must be certified by a licensed healthcare practitioner.
Rider 4 is the Children's Term Insurance Rider with a premium of $156 per year.
Rider 4 provides $15,000 coverage on each eligible child.
Rider 4 covers all natural and legally adopted children.
Rider 4 coverage applies from birth (or adoption) to age 25.
Rider 4 automatically includes future children.
No medical exam is required under Rider 4.
Children may convert to an individual policy at age 25 without evidence of insurability, up to 5 times the rider amount, under Rider 4.
Covered children under Rider 4 are Christopher Bennett Jr. (Age 8) and Olivia Grace Bennett (Age 5).
Rider 5 is the Accidental Death Benefit rider with a premium of $94 per year.
Rider 5 pays an additional $750,000 if death results from accidental injury.
Under Rider 5, the accident must be the direct cause of death.
Under Rider 5, death must occur within 90 days of the accident.
Rider 5 excludes suicide, self-inflicted injury, war, and aviation (as pilot/crew).
Rider 5 excludes deaths where the insured was under the influence of drugs or alcohol.
Rider 5 terminates at the insured's age 70.
Rider 6 is the Guaranteed Insurability Option rider with a premium of $89 per year.
Rider 6 allows purchase of up to $100,000 additional coverage per option date without medical underwriting.
Rider 6 option dates are at ages 40, 43, 46, 49, and at birth/adoption of a child.
The option under Rider 6 must be exercised within 90 days of the option date.
Under Rider 6, premium at exercise is based on age and class at time of exercise.
All Rider 6 options terminate at age 50.
The annual premium (base + all riders) is $1,089.00.
The premium due date is July 12 of each year.
The default payment mode is annual; monthly/quarterly is available upon request.
The grace period is 31 days from the due date.
Coverage continues during the grace period.
If death occurs during the grace period, unpaid premium will be deducted from the death benefit.
This policy is guaranteed renewable to age 95 at increased premiums shown in the renewal rate schedule.

The policy owner may convert all or part of this policy to permanent life insurance without evidence of insurability before the policy expiration date or before the insured's 70th birthday, whichever is earlier.
The minimum conversion amount is $50,000.
The insurer will not contest this policy after it has been in force for 2 years during the insured's lifetime, except for non-payment of premiums.
The standard aviation exclusion has been waived for this policy based on the insured's declared private pilot activities.
An additional premium of $175 per year for aviation waiver is included in the base premium.
Coverage under the aviation waiver includes death while operating or riding as a passenger in any aircraft, except military combat operations.
Riders may be added or removed on any policy anniversary with 30 days written notice.
Adding riders may require evidence of insurability.
Beneficiary changes may be made at any time by written request.
The policy was issued in Denver, Colorado.
The authorized signatory is the President and CEO of ProtectPlus Family Insurance Co.
The policy execution date is July 12, 2024.
Rachel Marie Bennett's relationship to the insured is Wife.
The 6 Activities of Daily Living under Rider 3 are: bathing, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring, and continence.
The base annual premium of $612.00 includes the $175/year aviation waiver premium.
The difference between total premium ($1,089) and base premium ($612) is $477, representing rider premiums.
The sum of individual rider premiums is: $102 + $0 + $48 + $156 + $94 + $89 = $489, plus $175 aviation = $664 total above base.
Rider 2 (Terminal Illness ADB) is included at no additional charge (free rider).
